name.en: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989
name.hi: अनुसूचित जाति और अनुसूचित जनजाति (अत्याचार निवारण) अधिनियम, 1989

applicable_situations: Caste-based abuse, humiliation, or assault against a member of SC or ST community by a non-SC/ST person
Forcing an SC/ST person to consume offensive substance or do forced labour
Wrongful occupation or dispossession of SC/ST land
Sexual exploitation or violence against an SC/ST woman with caste motivation
Boycott, threats, or intimidation aimed at preventing SC/ST persons from exercising rights
forum: Special Court designated under the Act in each district; investigation by an officer of rank not below DSP
limitation_period_note: FIR should be lodged promptly. Cognizance taken when investigation officer files chargesheet. The Act mandates investigation completion within 60 days.
procedural_steps: Lodge FIR at the nearest police station; police must register without delay (Section 18A)
Investigation by DSP-rank officer; chargesheet within 60 days
Trial in Special Court; victim and witness protection provisions apply
Relief and rehabilitation under Rules: immediate cash assistance, medical aid, transport, victim/witness protection
Appeal to High Court within 90 days of judgment
required_documents: Caste certificate of the SC/ST victim
Photographs / documentary evidence of the incident
